title: Drip vs Buttondown (2025): Which Is Better?
meta_description: Compare Drip and Buttondown email marketing tools. See pricing, features, and which suits e-commerce vs newsletter creators.
intro: Drip and Buttondown serve different email marketing needs. Drip specializes in e-commerce automation with revenue tracking, while Buttondown caters to developers and newsletter creators who value simplicity and markdown support. Understanding their distinct strengths helps you choose the right tool for your business.

comparison_table.tool_a: E-commerce tracking, revenue attribution, SMS, onsite campaigns
comparison_table.tool_b: Markdown writing, API access, paid subscriptions, simplicity

choose_a_if: You run an e-commerce business and need deep store integration with revenue tracking
You require SMS capabilities alongside email marketing
You want onsite campaigns and advanced segmentation for product-based businesses
choose_b_if: You're starting out and want to try email marketing free with up to 100 subscribers
You're a developer or technical writer who prefers writing in markdown
You need API access, paid subscriptions for readers, or multiple newsletters

faq.a: It depends on your needs. Drip excels for e-commerce businesses with advanced automation and revenue attribution, while Buttondown scores higher overall for simplicity, affordability, and user satisfaction. Buttondown's 4.8 G2 rating and 4.7 Trustpilot score outpace Drip's ratings, but Drip offers more robust e-commerce features.

faq.q: Which is cheaper, Drip or Buttondown?
faq.a: Buttondown is significantly cheaper, starting free for up to 100 subscribers and only $9/month for paid plans. Drip has no free tier and begins at $39/month. If cost is your primary concern, Buttondown is the clear winner, especially for small creators or startups.

faq.q: Can I switch from Buttondown to Drip?
faq.a: Yes, both platforms support import/export functionality, making migration possible. Buttondown explicitly lists import/export as a feature, and Drip can typically import subscriber lists. However, you'll lose some platform-specific automations and would need to rebuild workflows in Drip's visual builder.
